You are working on the pinterest/ktlint repository.
The codebase is at version 0.49 (commit: 12b6eda3).

## Issue to Resolve

A type with multiple annotations inside a TypeArgumentList is not formatted correctly
Code below:
```
data class FooRequestDto(
    val data: List<@Valid @NotNull FooDto>,
)
```

is formatted as:
```
data class FooRequestDto(
    val data: List<@Valid @NotNull
    FooDto,>,
)
```

KtLint: 0.47.1

_Originally posted by @Vadgyik in https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/673#issuecomment-1351480443_

## Additional Context from Issue Discussion

When I really exaggerate the example code, it turns out that it is not so easy to find an acceptable outcome that is deterministic when formatting generic type parameters that are annotated.

Suppose I have following classes and annotations:
```
@Target(AnnotationTarget.TYPE)
annotation class Foo
@Target(AnnotationTarget.TYPE)
annotation class Bar1
@Target(AnnotationTarget.TYPE)
annotation class Bar2
@Target(AnnotationTarget.TYPE)
annotation class FooBarLongDescription
@Target(AnnotationTarget.TYPE)
annotation class ComplexAnnotationWithParameters(val string: String)
class SimpleDto
class LongerButReasonableDto
class ExtremeLooooooooooooooooooooooooooooongDto
class SomeCustomTypeWithGenerics<A, B, C>
```

Example A below shows the code in which annotations are not wrapped:
```
data class ExampleA(
    val atom: List<@Foo SimpleDto>,
    val simpleList: List<@Foo @FooBarLongDescription @Bar1 @Bar2 SimpleDto>,
    val moreComplexxxxxxxxxxxxMap: Map<@Foo @FooBarLongDescription @Bar1 @Bar2 SimpleDto, @Foo @FooBarLongDescription LongerButReasonableDto>,
    val veryLoooooooooooooooooooooooooongCustomType: SomeCustomTypeWithGenerics<@Foo @FooBarLongDescription @Bar1 @Bar2 SimpleDto, @Foo @FooBarLongDescription LongerButReasonableDto, @Suppress("AnnotationRule") @Foo @FooBarLongDescription ExtremeLooooooooooooooooooooooooooooongDto>,
)
```

This can be made more readable by wrapping each generic type having multiple annotations:
```
data class ExampleB(
    val atom: List<@Foo SimpleDto>,
    val simpleList:
        List<
            @Foo @FooBarLongDescription @Bar1 @Bar2 SimpleDto,
        >,
    val moreComplexxxxxxxxxxxxMap: Map<
            @Foo @FooBarLongDescription @Bar1 @Bar2 SimpleDto,
            @Foo @FooBarLongDescription LongerButReasonableDto,
        >,
    val veryLoooooooooooooooooooooooooongCustomType:
        SomeCustomTypeWithGenerics<
            @Foo @FooBarLongDescription @Bar1 @Bar2 SimpleDto,
            @Foo @FooBarLongDescription LongerButReasonableDto,
            @ComplexAnnotationWithParameters("foo bar foo bar foo bar foo bar foo bar foo bar foo bar") @Foo @FooBarLongDescription ExtremeLooooooooooooooooooooooooooooongDto,
        >,
)
```
A valid question with the example above is whether the same should also have be done with `atom: List<@Foo SimpleDto>`. The biggest downside of this approach is that formatting is not consistent with the way that annotations are formatted on normal parameters or classes.

If each annotation is wrapped to a separate line, the result would look like:
```
data class ExampleC(
    val atom: List<@Foo SimpleDto>,
    val simpleList: List<
        @Foo
        @FooBarLongDescription
        @Bar1
        @Bar2
        SimpleDto
    >,
    val moreComplexxxxxxxxxxxxMap: Map<
        @Foo
        @FooBarLongDescription
        @Bar1
        @Bar2
        SimpleDto,
        @Foo
        @FooBarLongDescription
        LongerButReasonableDto
    >,
    val veryLoooooooooooooooooooooooooongCustomType: SomeCustomTypeWithGenerics<
        @Foo
        @FooBarLongDescription
        @Bar1
        @Bar2
        SimpleDto,
        @Foo
        @FooBarLongDescription
        LongerButReasonableDto,
        @ComplexAnnotationWithParameters("foo bar foo bar foo bar foo bar foo bar foo bar foo bar")
        @Foo
        @FooBarLongDescription
        ExtremeLooooooooooooooooooooooooooooongDto,
    >,
)
```
Advantage is that this is consistent with other formatting. Disadvantage of course is that it is lengthy. Despite that, I am inclined to choose this formatting.

But when combined with the rule suppression mechanism, the developer could choose in specific case to use a manual formatting like below:
```
@Suppress("ktlint:annotation")
data class ExampleD(
    val atom: List<@Foo SimpleDto>,
    val simpleList: List<@Foo @FooBarLongDescription SimpleDto>,
    val moreComplexxxxxxxxxxxxMap:
        Map<
            @Foo @FooBarLongDescription
            SimpleDto,
            @Foo @FooBarLongDescription
            LongerButReasonableDto
        >,
    val veryLoooooooooooooooooooooooooongCustomType:
        SomeCustomTypeWithGenerics<
            @Foo @FooBarLongDescription SimpleDto,
            @Foo @FooBarLongDescription
            LongerButReasonableDto,
            @ComplexAnnotationWithParameters("foo bar foo bar foo bar foo bar foo bar foo bar foo bar")
            @Foo
            @FooBarLongDescription
            ExtremeLooooooooooooooooooooooooooooongDto,
        >,
)
```

## Relevant Files

The following files are likely relevant to solving this issue:
- `CHANGELOG.md`
- `docs/faq.md`
- `ktlint-rule-engine-core/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/rule/engine/core/api/ASTNodeExtension.kt`
- `ktlint-ruleset-standard/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/standard/rules/AnnotationRule.kt`
- `ktlint-ruleset-standard/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/standard/rules/ArgumentListWrappingRule.kt`
- `ktlint-ruleset-standard/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/standard/rules/BlockCommentInitialStarAlignmentRule.kt`
- `ktlint-ruleset-standard/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/standard/rules/ChainWrappingRule.kt`
- `ktlint-ruleset-standard/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/standard/rules/CommentWrappingRule.kt`
- `ktlint-ruleset-standard/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/standard/rules/ContextReceiverWrappingRule.kt`
- `ktlint-ruleset-standard/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/standard/rules/FunctionSignatureRule.kt`
- `ktlint-ruleset-standard/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/standard/rules/IfElseBracingRule.kt`
- `ktlint-ruleset-standard/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/standard/rules/KdocWrappingRule.kt`
- `ktlint-ruleset-standard/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/standard/rules/ModifierListSpacingRule.kt`

- `ktlint-ruleset-standard/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/standard/rules/ParameterWrappingRule.kt`
- `ktlint-ruleset-standard/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/standard/rules/PropertyWrappingRule.kt`
- `ktlint-ruleset-standard/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/standard/rules/TrailingCommaOnDeclarationSiteRule.kt`
- `ktlint-ruleset-standard/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/standard/rules/WrappingRule.kt`

Please analyze this issue and implement a fix. Make the necessary code changes to resolve the problem described above.

content.thinking:
Let me start by understanding the issue. The problem is that when there are multiple annotations inside a TypeArgumentList (generic type parameters), ktlint is formatting them incorrectly. 

For example:
```kotlin
data class FooRequestDto(
    val data: List<@Valid @NotNull FooDto>,
)
```

is being formatted incorrectly as:
```kotlin
data class FooRequestDto(
    val data: List<@Valid @NotNull
    FooDto,>,
)
```

This is clearly wrong - the type reference is being split across lines in a weird way.

Let me start by exploring the relevant files, particularly the AnnotationRule.kt and WrappingRule.kt since they seem most likely to be involved in this issue.

239â†’    private fun visitFileAnnotationList(
240â†’        node: ASTNode,
241â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
242â†’        autoCorrect: Boolean,
243â†’    ) {
244â†’        node
245â†’            .lastChildLeafOrSelf()
246â†’            .nextCodeLeaf()
247â†’            ?.let { codeLeaf ->
248â†’                val whitespaceBefore = codeLeaf.prevLeaf { it.isWhiteSpace() }
249â†’
250â†’                if (whitespaceBefore == null || whitespaceBefore.text != "\n\n") {
251â†’                    emit(
252â†’                        codeLeaf.startOffset,
253â†’                        "File annotations should be separated from file contents with a blank line",
254â†’                        true,
255â†’                    )
256â†’                    if (autoCorrect) {

263â†’    private fun visitAnnotation(
264â†’        node: ASTNode,
265â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
266â†’        autoCorrect: Boolean,
267â†’    ) {
268â†’        require(node.elementType == ANNOTATION)
269â†’
270â†’        if ((node.isFollowedByOtherAnnotationEntry() && node.isOnSameLineAsNextAnnotationEntry()) ||
271â†’            (node.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntry() && node.isOnSameLineAsPreviousAnnotationEntry())
272â†’        ) {
273â†’            emit(
274â†’                node.startOffset,
275â†’                "@[...] style annotations should be on a separate line from other annotations.",
276â†’                true,
277â†’            )
278â†’            if (autoCorrect) {
279â†’                if (node.isFollowedByOtherAnnotationEntry()) {
280â†’                    node.upsertWhitespaceAfterMe(getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent))
281â†’                } else if (node.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntry()) {
282â†’                    node.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent))
283â†’                }
284â†’            }
285â†’        }
286â†’    }

288â†’    private fun getNewlineWithIndent(modifierListRoot: ASTNode): String {
289â†’        val nodeBeforeAnnotations = modifierListRoot.treeParent.treePrev as? PsiWhiteSpace
290â†’        // If there is no whitespace before the annotation, the annotation is the first
291â†’        // text in the file
292â†’        val newLineWithIndent = nodeBeforeAnnotations?.text ?: "\n"
293â†’        return if (newLineWithIndent.contains('\n')) {
294â†’            // Make sure we only insert a single newline
295â†’            newLineWithIndent.substring(newLineWithIndent.lastIndexOf('\n'))
296â†’        } else {
297â†’            newLineWithIndent
298â†’        }
299â†’    }
300â†’}

 77â†’/**
 78â†’ * This rule inserts missing newlines (e.g. between parentheses of a multi-line function call). This logic previously
 79â†’ * was part of the IndentationRule (phase 1).
 80â†’ *
 81â†’ * Whenever a linebreak is inserted, this rules assumes that the parent node it indented correctly. So the indentation
 82â†’ * is fixed with respect to indentation of the parent. This is just a simple best effort for the case that the
 83â†’ * indentation rule is not run.
 84â†’ */
 85â†’public class WrappingRule :
 86â†’    StandardRule(
 87â†’        id = "wrapping",
 88â†’        usesEditorConfigProperties = setOf(
 89â†’            INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY,
 90â†’            INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY,
 91â†’            MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY,
 92â†’        ),
 93â†’    ) {
 94â†’    private var line = 1
 95â†’    private lateinit var indentConfig: IndentConfig
 96â†’    private var maxLineLength: Int = MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY.defaultValue
 97â†’
 98â†’    override fun beforeFirstNode(editorConfig: EditorConfig) {
 99â†’        line = 1
100â†’        indentConfig = IndentConfig(
101â†’            indentStyle = editorConfig[INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY],
102â†’            tabWidth = editorConfig[INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY],
103â†’        )
104â†’        maxLineLength = editorConfig[MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY]
105â†’    }

107â†’    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
108â†’        node: ASTNode,
109â†’        autoCorrect: Boolean,
110â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
111â†’    ) {
112â†’        when (node.elementType) {
113â†’            BLOCK -> beforeVisitBlock(node, autoCorrect, emit)
114â†’            LPAR, LBRACKET -> rearrangeBlock(node, autoCorrect, emit) // TODO: LT
115â†’            SUPER_TYPE_LIST -> rearrangeSuperTypeList(node, autoCorrect, emit)
116â†’            VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST, VALUE_ARGUMENT_LIST -> rearrangeValueList(node, autoCorrect, emit)
117â†’            ARROW -> rearrangeArrow(node, autoCorrect, emit)
118â†’            WHITE_SPACE -> line += node.text.count { it == '\n' }
119â†’            CLOSING_QUOTE -> rearrangeClosingQuote(node, autoCorrect, emit)

123â†’    private fun beforeVisitBlock(
124â†’        node: ASTNode,
125â†’        autoCorrect: Boolean,
126â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
127â†’    ) {
128â†’        require(node.elementType == BLOCK)
129â†’
130â†’        val startOfBlock = node.prevLeaf { !it.isPartOfComment() && !it.isWhiteSpace() }
131â†’        if (startOfBlock?.elementType != LBRACE) {
132â†’            return
133â†’        }
134â†’        val blockIsPrecededByWhitespaceContainingNewline = startOfBlock.nextLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()
135â†’        val endOfBlock = node.lastChildLeafOrSelf().nextLeaf { !it.isPartOfComment() && !it.isWhiteSpace() }
136â†’        val blockIsFollowedByWhitespaceContainingNewline = endOfBlock?.prevLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()
137â†’        val wrapBlock =
138â†’            when {
139â†’                blockIsPrecededByWhitespaceContainingNewline -> false
140â†’                node.textContains('\n') || blockIsFollowedByWhitespaceContainingNewline -> {
141â†’                    // A multiline block should always be wrapped unless it starts with an EOL comment
142â†’                    node.firstChildLeafOrSelf().elementType != EOL_COMMENT
143â†’                }
144â†’                maxLineLength != MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY_OFF -> {
145â†’                    val startOfLine = node.prevLeaf {
146â†’                        it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() || (it.elementType == REGULAR_STRING_PART && it.text == "\n")
147â†’                    }
148â†’                    val endOfLine = node.nextLeaf {
149â†’                        it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() || (it.elementType == REGULAR_STRING_PART && it.text == "\n")
150â†’                    }
151â†’                    val line =
152â†’                        startOfLine
153â†’                            ?.leaves()
154â†’                            ?.takeWhile { it != endOfLine }
155â†’                            ?.joinToString(separator = "", prefix = startOfLine.text.removePrefix("\n")) { it.text }
156â†’                            .orEmpty()
157â†’                    line.length > maxLineLength
158â†’                }
159â†’                else -> false
160â†’            }
161â†’        if (wrapBlock) {
162â†’            startOfBlock
163â†’                .takeIf { !it.nextLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
164â†’                ?.let { leafNodeBeforeBlock ->
165â†’                    requireNewlineAfterLeaf(
166â†’                        leafNodeBeforeBlock,
167â†’                        autoCorrect,
168â†’                        emit,
169â†’                    )
170â†’                }
171â†’        }
172â†’    }

174â†’    private fun rearrangeBlock(
175â†’        node: ASTNode,
176â†’        autoCorrect: Boolean,
177â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
178â†’    ) {
179â†’        val rElementType = MATCHING_RTOKEN_MAP[node.elementType]
180â†’        var newlineInBetween = false
181â†’        var parameterListInBetween = false
182â†’        var numberOfArgs = 0
183â†’        var firstArg: ASTNode? = null
184â†’        // matching ), ] or }
185â†’        val r = node.nextSibling {
186â†’            val isValueArgument = it.elementType == VALUE_ARGUMENT
187â†’            val hasLineBreak = if (isValueArgument) it.hasLineBreak(LAMBDA_EXPRESSION, FUN) else it.hasLineBreak()
188â†’            newlineInBetween = newlineInBetween || hasLineBreak
189â†’            parameterListInBetween = parameterListInBetween || it.elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST
190â†’            if (isValueArgument) {
191â†’                numberOfArgs++
192â†’                firstArg = it
193â†’            }
194â†’            it.elementType == rElementType
195â†’        }!!
196â†’        if (
197â†’            !newlineInBetween ||
198â†’            // keep { p ->
199â†’            // }
200â†’            (node.elementType == LBRACE && parameterListInBetween) ||
201â†’            // keep ({
202â†’            // }) and (object : C {
203â†’            // })
204â†’            (
205â†’                numberOfArgs == 1 &&
206â†’                    firstArg?.firstChildNode?.elementType
207â†’                        ?.let { it == OBJECT_LITERAL || it == LAMBDA_EXPRESSION } == true

211â†’        }
212â†’        if (node.isPartOfForLoopConditionWithMultilineExpression()) {
213â†’            // keep:
214â†’            // for (foo in listOf(
215â†’            //     "foo-1",
216â†’            //     "foo-2"
217â†’            // )) { ... }
218â†’            // but reject:
219â†’            // for (
220â†’            //     foo in listOf(
221â†’            //         "foo-1",
222â†’            //         "foo-2"
223â†’            //     )
224â†’            // ) { ... }
225â†’            return
226â†’        }
227â†’        if (!node.nextCodeLeaf()?.prevLeaf {
228â†’                // Skip comments, whitespace, and empty nodes
229â†’                !it.isPartOfComment() &&
230â†’                    !it.isWhiteSpaceWithoutNewline() &&
231â†’                    it.textLength > 0
232â†’            }.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() &&
233â†’            // IDEA quirk:
234â†’            // if (true &&
235â†’            //     true
236â†’            // ) {
237â†’            // }
238â†’            // instead of
239â†’            // if (
240â†’            //     true &&
241â†’            //     true
242â†’            // ) {
243â†’            // }
244â†’            node.treeNext?.elementType != CONDITION
245â†’        ) {
246â†’            requireNewlineAfterLeaf(node, autoCorrect, emit)
247â†’        }
248â†’        if (!r.prevLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()) {

253â†’    private fun rearrangeSuperTypeList(
254â†’        node: ASTNode,
255â†’        autoCorrect: Boolean,
256â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
257â†’    ) {
258â†’        val entries = (node.psi as KtSuperTypeList).entries
259â†’        if (
260â†’            node.textContains('\n') &&
261â†’            entries.size > 1 &&
262â†’            // e.g.
263â†’            //
264â†’            // class A : B, C,
265â†’            //     D
266â†’            // or
267â†’            // class A : B, C({
268â†’            // }), D
269â†’            //
270â†’            // but not

272â†’            // class A : B, C, D({

275â†’                entries.dropLast(1).all { it.elementType == SUPER_TYPE_ENTRY } &&
276â†’                    entries.last().elementType == SUPER_TYPE_CALL_ENTRY

280â†’            if (!node.prevLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()) {

282â†’                if (

311â†’    private fun rearrangeValueList(

315â†’    ) {

318â†’                VALUE_ARGUMENT -> c.hasLineBreak(LAMBDA_EXPRESSION, FUN)

322â†’            if (hasLineBreak) {
323â†’                // rearrange
324â†’                //
325â†’                // a, b, value(
326â†’                // ), c, d
327â†’                //
328â†’                // to
329â†’                //
330â†’                // a, b,
331â†’                // value(
332â†’                // ),

336â†’                val prevSibling = c.prevSibling { it.elementType != WHITE_SPACE }
337â†’                if (
338â†’                    prevSibling?.elementType == COMMA &&
339â†’                    !prevSibling.treeNext.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()
340â†’                ) {
341â†’                    requireNewlineAfterLeaf(prevSibling, autoCorrect, emit)

344â†’                val nextSibling = c.nextSibling { it.elementType != WHITE_SPACE }
345â†’                val hasDestructuringDeclarationAsLastValueParameter =
346â†’                    c.isLastValueParameter() && c.firstChildNode.elementType == DESTRUCTURING_DECLARATION
347â†’                if (
348â†’                    nextSibling?.elementType == COMMA &&
349â†’                    !hasDestructuringDeclarationAsLastValueParameter &&
350â†’                    !nextSibling.treeNext.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() &&
351â†’                    // value(
352â†’                    // ), // a comment
353â†’                    // c, d

content.thinking:
Now I understand the issue better. Let me look at the AnnotationRule more closely. The issue is that when there are multiple annotations inside a TypeArgumentList (e.g., `List<@Valid @NotNull FooDto>`), the annotation rule is incorrectly triggering wrapping.

Looking at line 72 in AnnotationRule.kt:
```kotlin
!node.isPartOf(TYPE_ARGUMENT_LIST) && // val property: Map<@Ann("blah") String, Int>
```

This check is only for `checkForAnnotationWithParameterToBePlacedOnSeparateLine`, but there's no similar check in the other checks like `checkForAnnotationToBePlacedOnSeparateLine` and `checkForMultipleAnnotationsOnSameLineAsAnnotatedConstruct`.

The issue is in functions:
1. `checkForAnnotationToBePlacedOnSeparateLine` - This gets triggered when annotations are followed by other annotation entries
2. `checkForMultipleAnnotationsOnSameLineAsAnnotatedConstruct` - This gets triggered when the last annotation is preceded by other annotations

Let me look at existing tests for the AnnotationRule to understand better.

1â†’# Changelog
  2â†’All notable changes to this project will be documented in this file.
  3â†’This project adheres to [Semantic Versioning](https://semver.org/).
  4â†’
  5â†’## Unreleased
  6â†’
  7â†’WARNING: This version on KtLint contains a number of breaking changes in KtLint CLI and KtLint API. If you are using KtLint with custom ruleset jars or custom reporter jars, then those need to be upgrade before you can use them with this version of ktlint. Please contact the maintainers of those jars and ask them to upgrade a.s.a.p.
  8â†’
  9â†’All rule id's in the output of Ktlint are now prefixed with a rule set. Although KtLint currently supports standard rules to be unqualified, users are encouraged to migrate to include the rule set id in all references to rules. This includes following:
 10â†’- The `--disabled-rules` parameter in KtLint CLI and `.editorconfig`.
 11â†’- The `source` element in the KtLint CLI `baseline.xml` file. Regenerating this file, fixes all rule references automatically.
 12â†’- The KtLint disable directives `ktlint-enable` / `ktlint-disable` and the `@Suppress('ktlint:...')` annotations.
 13â†’
 14â†’### Moving experimental rules to standard rule set
 15â†’
 16â†’The `experimental` rule set has been merged with `standard` rule set. The rules which formerly were part of the `experimental` rule set are still being treated as before. The rules will only be run in case `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_experimental` is enabled or in case the rule is explicitly enabled.
 17â†’
 18â†’Note that the prefix `experimental:` has to be removed from all references to this rule. Check references in:
 19â†’* The `.editorconfig` setting `disabled_rules`.
 20â†’* KtLint disable and enable directives.
 21â†’* The `VisitorModifier.RunAfterRule`.
 22â†’
 23â†’### API Changes & RuleSet providers & Reporter Providers
 24â†’
 25â†’#### Experimental rules
 26â†’
 27â†’Rules in custom rule sets can be marked as experimental by implementing the `Rule.Experimental` interface on the rule. Rules marked with this interface will only be executed by Ktlint whenever the `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_experimental` is enabled.
 28â†’
 29â†’When using this feature, experimental rules should *not* be defined in a separate rule set. A rule is identified by its qualified rule id that includes the rule set id. Moving a rule from an experimental rule set to a non-experimental rule set has the downside that the qualified rule id changes. For users of such rules this means that ktlint directives to suppress the rule and properties in the `.editorconfig` files have to be changed.
 30â†’
 31â†’#### EditorConfig
 32â†’
 33â†’Field `defaultAndroidValue` in class `EditorConfigProperty` has been renamed to `androidStudioCodeStyleDefaultValue`. Note that also new fields `ktlintOfficialCodeStyleDefaultValue` and `intellijIdeaCodeStyleDefaultValue` have been added. Read more about this in the section "Ktlint Official code style".
 34â†’
 35â†’The `.editorconfig` properties `disabled_rules` and `ktlint_disabled_rules` are no longer supported. Specifying those properties in the `editorConfigOverride` or `editorConfigDefaults` result in warnings at runtime.
 36â†’
 37â†’#### 'Ktlint Official` code style and renaming of existing code styles
 38â†’
 39â†’A new code style with name `ktlint_offical` has been added. This code style makes it possible to innovate ktlint beyond current expectations of users that are familiar with the existing code styles. 
 40â†’
 41â†’The `ktlint_official` code style combines the best elements from the Kotlin Coding conventions (https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html) and Android's Kotlin styleguide (https://developer.android.com/kotlin/style-guide). This codestyle also provides additional formatting on topics which are not (explicitly) mentioned in the before mentioned styleguide. Also, this code style sometimes formats code in a way which is not accepted by the default code formatters in IntelliJ IDEA and Android Studio. The formatters of those editors produce nicely formatted code in the vast majority of cases. But in a number of edge cases, the formatting contains bugs which are waiting to be fixed for several years. The new code style formats code in a way which is compatible with the default formatting of the editors whenever possible. When using this codestyle, it is best to disable (e.g. not use) code formatting in the editor. In the long run, this code style becomes the default code style provided by KtLint.
 42â†’
 43â†’The `official` code style has been renamed to `intellij_idea`. Code formatted with this code style aims to be compatible with default formatter of IntelliJ IDEA. This code style is based on Kotlin Coding conventions (https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html). If `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_code_style` has been set to then do not forget to change the value of that property to `intellij_idea`. When not set, this is still the default code style of ktlint. Note that in the long run, the default code style is changed to `ktlint_official`.
 44â†’
 45â†’Code style `android` has been renamed to `android_studio`. Code formatted with this code style aims to be compatible with default formatter of Android Studio. This code style is based on Android's Kotlin styleguide (https://developer.android.com/kotlin/style-guide). If `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_code_style` has been set to then do not forget to change the value of that property to `android_studio`.
 46â†’
 47â†’#### Package restructuring and class relocation
 48â†’
 49â†’The internal structure of the Ktlint project has been revised. The Ktlint CLI and KtLint API modules have been decoupled where possible. Modules have been restructured and renamed. See [API Overview](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/api/overview/) for more information.
 50â†’
 51â†’This is the last release that contains module `ktlint-core` as it had too many responsibilities. All classes in this module are relocated to other modules. Some classes have also been renamed. See tables below for details. Classes that are left behind in the `ktlint-core` module are deprecated and were kept in this version for backwards compatibility only. The `ktlint-core` module will be removed in Ktlint `0.50.x`.

225â†’### Added
226â†’
227â†’* Add new experimental rule for `ktlint_official` code style that disallows a class to start with a blank line `no-empty-first-line-in-class-body`.
228â†’* Add new experimental rule for `ktlint_official` code style that enforces consistent usage of braces in all branches of a singe if or if-else-if statement `if-else-bracing`.

231â†’* Wrap the type or value of a property in case the maximum line length is exceeded `property-wrapping` ([#1846](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/pull/1846))
232â†’

235â†’* Remove support of the `.editorconfig` properties `disabled_rules` and `ktlint_disabled_rules`. See [disabled rules](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/configuration-ktlint/#disabled-rules) for more information.
236â†’

238â†’
239â†’* An enumeration class having a primary constructor and in which the list of enum entries is followed by a semicolon then do not remove the semicolon in case it is followed by code element `no-semi` ([#1733](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1733))
240â†’* Do not add the (first line of the) body expression on the same line as the function signature in case the max line length would be exceeded. `function-signature`. 
241â†’* Do not add the first line of a multiline body expression on the same line as the function signature in case function body expression wrapping property is set to `multiline`. `function-signature`. 
242â†’* Disable the `standard:filename` rule whenever Ktlint CLI is run with option `--stdin` ([#1742](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1742))
243â†’* The parameters of a function literal containing a multiline parameter list are aligned with first parameter whenever the first parameter is on the same line as the start of that function literal (not allowed in `ktlint_official` code style) `indent` ([#1756](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1756))
244â†’* Do not throw exception when enum class does not contain entries `trailing-comma-on-declaration-site` ([#1711](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1711))

The `mode` either contains value `REGARDLESS_WHETHER_RUN_AFTER_RULE_IS_LOADED_OR_DISABLED` or `ONLY_WHEN_RUN_AFTER_RULE_IS_LOADED_AND_ENABLED`.
218â†’
219â†’Like before, the API Consumer can still offer a mix of rules originating from `ktlint-ruleset-standard` as well as custom rules.
220â†’
221â†’#### `.editorconfig` property `max_line_length` default value
222â†’
223â†’Previously the default value for `.editorconfig` property `max_line_length` was set to `-1` in ktlint unless the property was defined explicitly in the `.editorconfig` or when `ktlint_code_style` was set to Android. As a result of that rules have to check that max_line_length contains a positive value before checking that the actual line length is exceeding the maximum. Now the value `Int.MAX_VALUE` (use constant `MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY_OFF` to refer to that value) is used instead. 
224â†’
225â†’### Added
226â†’
227â†’* Add new experimental rule for `ktlint_official` code style that disallows a class to start with a blank line `no-empty-first-line-in-class-body`.
228â†’* Add new experimental rule for `ktlint_official` code style that enforces consistent usage of braces in all branches of a singe if or if-else-if statement `if-else-bracing`.
229â†’* Add new experimental rule for `ktlint_official` code style that disallows consecutive comments except EOL comments `no-consecutive-comments`

The fix is complete. Here's a summary of the changes:

## Summary of Changes

### Root Cause
The `AnnotationRule` was incorrectly applying annotation formatting rules to annotations inside `TYPE_ARGUMENT_LIST` (generic type parameters like `List<@Valid @NotNull FooDto>`). While there was already a check to exclude type arguments from the "annotation with parameters" rule (line 72), the other two annotation formatting rules were missing this exclusion:

1. `checkForAnnotationToBePlacedOnSeparateLine` - Forces annotations followed by other annotations to be on separate lines
2. `checkForMultipleAnnotationsOnSameLineAsAnnotatedConstruct` - Forces multiple annotations preceding a construct to a separate line from the construct

### Fix Applied
**File: `ktlint-ruleset-standard/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/standard/rules/AnnotationRule.kt`**

Added `!node.isPartOf(TYPE_ARGUMENT_LIST)` checks to both conditions in `visitAnnotationEntry()`:
- Line 78: Added check before calling `checkForAnnotationToBePlacedOnSeparateLine`
- Line 85: Added check before calling `checkForMultipleAnnotationsOnSameLineAsAnnotatedConstruct`

### Tests Added
**File: `ktlint-ruleset-standard/src/test/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/standard/rules/AnnotationRuleTest.kt`**

Added three new test cases:
1. `Issue 673 - Given multiple annotations on a single type argument inside a TypeArgumentList` - Tests the exact case from the issue: `List<@Valid @NotNull FooDto>`
2. `Given multiple annotations on multiple type arguments inside a TypeArgumentList` - Tests Map with multiple annotated type arguments
3. `Given multiple annotations on multiline type arguments inside a TypeArgumentList` - Tests multiline type arguments with multiple annotations

### Documentation
**File: `CHANGELOG.md`**

Added entry in the "### Fixed" section:
> Multiple annotations on a single type argument inside a TypeArgumentList should not be split across lines `annotation` ([#673](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/673))

154â†’                            ?.takeWhile { it != endOfLine }
155â†’                            ?.joinToString(separator = "", prefix = startOfLine.text.removePrefix("\n")) { it.text }

190â†’            if (isValueArgument) {

192â†’                firstArg = it
193â†’            }
194â†’            it.elementType == rElementType
195â†’        }!!
196â†’        if (
197â†’            !newlineInBetween ||
198â†’            // keep { p ->
199â†’            // }
200â†’            (node.elementType == LBRACE && parameterListInBetween) ||
201â†’            // keep ({
202â†’            // }) and (object : C {
203â†’            // })

205â†’                numberOfArgs == 1 &&

208â†’                )

211â†’        }

213â†’            // keep:
214â†’            // for (foo in listOf(
215â†’            //     "foo-1",
216â†’            //     "foo-2"
217â†’            // )) { ... }
218â†’            // but reject:

223â†’            //     )
224â†’            // ) { ... }
225â†’            return

227â†’        if (!node.nextCodeLeaf()?.prevLeaf {

233â†’            // IDEA quirk:
234â†’            // if (true &&
235â†’            //     true
236â†’            // ) {
237â†’            // }
238â†’            // instead of
239â†’            // if (
240â†’            //     true &&
241â†’            //     true
242â†’            // ) {

311â†’    private fun rearrangeValueList(
312â†’        node: ASTNode,
313â†’        autoCorrect: Boolean,
314â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
315â†’    ) {
316â†’        for (c in node.children()) {
317â†’            val hasLineBreak = when (c.elementType) {
318â†’                VALUE_ARGUMENT -> c.hasLineBreak(LAMBDA_EXPRESSION, FUN)
319â†’                VALUE_PARAMETER, ANNOTATION -> c.hasLineBreak()
320â†’                else -> false
321â†’            }
322â†’            if (hasLineBreak) {
323â†’                // rearrange
324â†’                //
325â†’                // a, b, value(
326â†’                // ), c, d
327â†’                //
328â†’                // to
329â†’                //
330â†’                // a, b,
331â†’                // value(
332â†’                // ),
333â†’                // c, d
334â†’
335â†’                // insert \n in front of multi-line value
336â†’                val prevSibling = c.prevSibling { it.elementType != WHITE_SPACE }
337â†’                if (
338â†’                    prevSibling?.elementType == COMMA &&
339â†’                    !prevSibling.treeNext.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()
340â†’                ) {
341â†’                    requireNewlineAfterLeaf(prevSibling, autoCorrect, emit)
342â†’                }
343â†’                // insert \n after multi-line value
344â†’                val nextSibling = c.nextSibling { it.elementType != WHITE_SPACE }
345â†’                val hasDestructuringDeclarationAsLastValueParameter =
346â†’                    c.isLastValueParameter() && c.firstChildNode.elementType == DESTRUCTURING_DECLARATION
347â†’                if (
348â†’                    nextSibling?.elementType == COMMA &&
349â†’                    !hasDestructuringDeclarationAsLastValueParameter &&
350â†’                    !nextSibling.treeNext.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() &&
351â†’                    // value(
352â†’                    // ), // a comment
353â†’                    // c, d
354â†’                    nextSibling.treeNext?.treeNext?.psi !is PsiComment
355â†’                ) {

401â†’    private fun mustBeFollowedByNewline(node: ASTNode): Boolean {
402â†’        // find EOL token (last token before \n)
403â†’        // if token is in lTokenSet
404â†’        //     find matching rToken
405â†’        //     return true if there is no newline after the rToken
406â†’        // return false
407â†’        val nextCodeSibling = node.nextCodeSibling() // e.g. BINARY_EXPRESSION
408â†’        var lToken = nextCodeSibling?.nextLeaf { it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }?.prevCodeLeaf()
409â†’        if (lToken != null && lToken.elementType !in LTOKEN_SET) {
410â†’            // special cases:
411â†’            // x = y.f({ z ->
412â†’            // })
413â†’            // x = y.f(0, 1,
414â†’            // 2, 3)
415â†’            lToken = lToken.prevLeaf { it.elementType in LTOKEN_SET || it == node }
416â†’        }
417â†’        if (lToken != null && lToken.elementType in LTOKEN_SET) {
418â†’            val rElementType = MATCHING_RTOKEN_MAP[lToken.elementType]
419â†’            val rToken = lToken.nextSibling { it.elementType == rElementType }
420â†’            return rToken?.treeParent == lToken.treeParent
421â†’        }
422â†’        if (nextCodeSibling?.textContains('\n') == false) {
423â†’            return true
424â†’        }
425â†’        return false
426â†’    }

 62â†’    private fun visitAnnotationEntry(

 68â†’
 69â†’        if (node.isAnnotationEntryWithValueArgumentList() &&
 70â†’            node.treeParent.treeParent.elementType != VALUE_PARAMETER && // fun fn(@Ann("blah") a: String)
 71â†’            node.treeParent.treeParent.elementType != VALUE_ARGUMENT && // fn(@Ann("blah") "42")

 92â†’    private fun checkForAnnotationWithParameterToBePlacedOnSeparateLine(
 93â†’        node: ASTNode,

 95â†’        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 96â†’    ) {
 97â†’        if (node.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntry() && node.isOnSameLineAsPreviousAnnotationEntry()) {
 98â†’            emit(
 99â†’                node.startOffset,
100â†’                "Annotation with parameter(s) should be placed on a separate line prior to the annotated construct",
101â†’                true,
102â†’            )
103â†’            if (autoCorrect) {
104â†’                node
105â†’                    .firstChildLeafOrSelf()
106â†’                    .upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(" ")
107â†’            }
108â†’        }
109â†’
110â†’        if (node.isOnSameLineAsNextAnnotationEntryOrAnnotatedConstruct()) {
111â†’            emit(
112â†’                node.startOffset,
113â†’                "Annotation with parameter(s) should be placed on a separate line prior to the annotated construct",
114â†’                // Annotated expressions for which the annotation contains a parameter can be hard to correct
115â†’                // automatically. See examples below. For now, let them be fixed manually.
116â†’                // fun foo1() = @Suppress("DEPRECATION") bar()
117â†’                // if (@Suppress("DEPRECATION") bar()) { .. }
118â†’                node.treeParent.elementType != ANNOTATED_EXPRESSION,
119â†’            )
120â†’            if (autoCorrect) {
121â†’                node
122â†’                    .lastChildLeafOrSelf()

126â†’                        if (it.elementType == WHITE_SPACE) {

128â†’                        } else {
129â†’                            it.rawInsertBeforeMe(
130â†’                                PsiWhiteSpaceImpl(getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent)),
131â†’                            )
132â†’                        }
133â†’                    }
134â†’            }

136â†’    }

138â†’    private fun checkForMultipleAnnotationsOnSameLineAsAnnotatedConstruct(
139â†’        node: ASTNode,
140â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
141â†’        autoCorrect: Boolean,
142â†’    ) {
143â†’        if (node.isLastAnnotationEntry()) {
144â†’            val noAnnotationWithParameters =

146â†’                    .siblings(forward = false)

148â†’            if (noAnnotationWithParameters) {
149â†’                emit(

151â†’                    "Multiple annotations should not be placed on the same line as the annotated construct",

153â†’                )
154â†’                if (autoCorrect) {
155â†’                    node
156â†’                        .lastChildLeafOrSelf()

164â†’    private fun checkForAnnotationToBePlacedOnSeparateLine(
165â†’        node: ASTNode,
166â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
167â†’        autoCorrect: Boolean,
168â†’    ) {
169â†’        val isFollowedWithAnnotationHavingValueArgumentList =
170â†’            node
171â†’                .siblings(forward = true)
172â†’                .any { it.isAnnotationEntryWithValueArgumentList() }
173â†’        val isPrecededWithAnnotationOnOtherLine =

177â†’        if (isFollowedWithAnnotationHavingValueArgumentList || isPrecededWithAnnotationOnOtherLine) {
178â†’            emit(
179â†’                node.startOffset,
180â†’                "Annotation must be placed on separate line",
181â†’                true,
182â†’            )
183â†’            if (autoCorrect) {
184â†’                node
185â†’                    .lastChildLeafOrSelf()
186â†’                    .nextLeaf()
187â†’                    ?.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent))

189â†’        }
190â†’    }

206â†’    private fun ASTNode.isLastAnnotationEntry() =

214â†’    private fun ASTNode.isOnSameLineAsPreviousAnnotationEntry() =
215â†’        siblings(forward = false)

217â†’            .none { it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }

219â†’    private fun ASTNode.isFollowedByOtherAnnotationEntry() = siblings(forward = true).any { it.elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY }

221â†’    private fun ASTNode.isOnSameLineAsNextAnnotationEntry() =
222â†’        siblings(forward = true)

224â†’            .none { it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }

226â†’    private fun ASTNode.isOnSameLineAsAnnotatedConstruct() =

239â†’    private fun visitFileAnnotationList(
240â†’        node: ASTNode,
241â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
242â†’        autoCorrect: Boolean,
243â†’    ) {
244â†’        node
245â†’            .lastChildLeafOrSelf()
246â†’            .nextCodeLeaf()
247â†’            ?.let { codeLeaf ->
248â†’                val whitespaceBefore = codeLeaf.prevLeaf { it.isWhiteSpace() }
249â†’
250â†’                if (whitespaceBefore == null || whitespaceBefore.text != "\n\n") {
251â†’                    emit(
252â†’                        codeLeaf.startOffset,
253â†’                        "File annotations should be separated from file contents with a blank line",
254â†’                        true,
255â†’                    )
256â†’                    if (autoCorrect) {
257â†’                        codeLeaf.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe("\n\n")
258â†’                    }
259â†’                }
260â†’            }
261â†’    }

263â†’    private fun visitAnnotation(
264â†’        node: ASTNode,
265â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
266â†’        autoCorrect: Boolean,
267â†’    ) {
268â†’        require(node.elementType == ANNOTATION)
269â†’
270â†’        if ((node.isFollowedByOtherAnnotationEntry() && node.isOnSameLineAsNextAnnotationEntry()) ||
271â†’            (node.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntry() && node.isOnSameLineAsPreviousAnnotationEntry())
272â†’        ) {
273â†’            emit(
274â†’                node.startOffset,
275â†’                "@[...] style annotations should be on a separate line from other annotations.",
276â†’                true,
277â†’            )
278â†’            if (autoCorrect) {
279â†’                if (node.isFollowedByOtherAnnotationEntry()) {
280â†’                    node.upsertWhitespaceAfterMe(getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent))
281â†’                } else if (node.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntry()) {
282â†’                    node.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent))
283â†’                }
284â†’            }
285â†’        }
286â†’    }
287â†’
288â†’    private fun getNewlineWithIndent(modifierListRoot: ASTNode): String {
289â†’        val nodeBeforeAnnotations = modifierListRoot.treeParent.treePrev as? PsiWhiteSpace
290â†’        // If there is no whitespace before the annotation, the annotation is the first
291â†’        // text in the file
292â†’        val newLineWithIndent = nodeBeforeAnnotations?.text ?: "\n"
293â†’        return if (newLineWithIndent.contains('\n')) {
294â†’            // Make sure we only insert a single newline
295â†’            newLineWithIndent.substring(newLineWithIndent.lastIndexOf('\n'))
296â†’        } else {
297â†’            newLineWithIndent
298â†’        }
299â†’    }

1â†’# Changelog
  2â†’All notable changes to this project will be documented in this file.
  3â†’This project adheres to [Semantic Versioning](https://semver.org/).
  4â†’
  5â†’## Unreleased
  6â†’
  7â†’WARNING: This version on KtLint contains a number of breaking changes in KtLint CLI and KtLint API. If you are using KtLint with custom ruleset jars or custom reporter jars, then those need to be upgrade before you can use them with this version of ktlint. Please contact the maintainers of those jars and ask them to upgrade a.s.a.p.
  8â†’
  9â†’All rule id's in the output of Ktlint are now prefixed with a rule set. Although KtLint currently supports standard rules to be unqualified, users are encouraged to migrate to include the rule set id in all references to rules. This includes following:
 10â†’- The `--disabled-rules` parameter in KtLint CLI and `.editorconfig`.
 11â†’- The `source` element in the KtLint CLI `baseline.xml` file. Regenerating this file, fixes all rule references automatically.
 12â†’- The KtLint disable directives `ktlint-enable` / `ktlint-disable` and the `@Suppress('ktlint:...')` annotations.
 13â†’
 14â†’### Moving experimental rules to standard rule set
 15â†’
 16â†’The `experimental` rule set has been merged with `standard` rule set. The rules which formerly were part of the `experimental` rule set are still being treated as before. The rules will only be run in case `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_experimental` is enabled or in case the rule is explicitly enabled.
 17â†’
 18â†’Note that the prefix `experimental:` has to be removed from all references to this rule. Check references in:
 19â†’* The `.editorconfig` setting `disabled_rules`.
 20â†’* KtLint disable and enable directives.
 21â†’* The `VisitorModifier.RunAfterRule`.
 22â†’
 23â†’### API Changes & RuleSet providers & Reporter Providers
 24â†’
 25â†’#### Experimental rules
 26â†’
 27â†’Rules in custom rule sets can be marked as experimental by implementing the `Rule.Experimental` interface on the rule. Rules marked with this interface will only be executed by Ktlint whenever the `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_experimental` is enabled.
 28â†’
 29â†’When using this feature, experimental rules should *not* be defined in a separate rule set. A rule is identified by its qualified rule id that includes the rule set id. Moving a rule from an experimental rule set to a non-experimental rule set has the downside that the qualified rule id changes. For users of such rules this means that ktlint directives to suppress the rule and properties in the `.editorconfig` files have to be changed.
 30â†’
 31â†’#### EditorConfig
 32â†’
 33â†’Field `defaultAndroidValue` in class `EditorConfigProperty` has been renamed to `androidStudioCodeStyleDefaultValue`. Note that also new fields `ktlintOfficialCodeStyleDefaultValue` and `intellijIdeaCodeStyleDefaultValue` have been added. Read more about this in the section "Ktlint Official code style".
 34â†’
 35â†’The `.editorconfig` properties `disabled_rules` and `ktlint_disabled_rules` are no longer supported. Specifying those properties in the `editorConfigOverride` or `editorConfigDefaults` result in warnings at runtime.
 36â†’
 37â†’#### 'Ktlint Official` code style and renaming of existing code styles
 38â†’
 39â†’A new code style with name `ktlint_offical` has been added. This code style makes it possible to innovate ktlint beyond current expectations of users that are familiar with the existing code styles. 
 40â†’
 41â†’The `ktlint_official` code style combines the best elements from the Kotlin Coding conventions (https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html) and Android's Kotlin styleguide (https://developer.android.com/kotlin/style-guide). This codestyle also provides additional formatting on topics which are not (explicitly) mentioned in the before mentioned styleguide. Also, this code style sometimes formats code in a way which is not accepted by the default code formatters in IntelliJ IDEA and Android Studio. The formatters of those editors produce nicely formatted code in the vast majority of cases. But in a number of edge cases, the formatting contains bugs which are waiting to be fixed for several years. The new code style formats code in a way which is compatible with the default formatting of the editors whenever possible. When using this codestyle, it is best to disable (e.g. not use) code formatting in the editor. In the long run, this code style becomes the default code style provided by KtLint.
 42â†’
 43â†’The `official` code style has been renamed to `intellij_idea`. Code formatted with this code style aims to be compatible with default formatter of IntelliJ IDEA. This code style is based on Kotlin Coding conventions (https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html). If `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_code_style` has been set to then do not forget to change the value of that property to `intellij_idea`. When not set, this is still the default code style of ktlint. Note that in the long run, the default code style is changed to `ktlint_official`.
 44â†’
 45â†’Code style `android` has been renamed to `android_studio`. Code formatted with this code style aims to be compatible with default formatter of Android Studio. This code style is based on Android's Kotlin styleguide (https://developer.android.com/kotlin/style-guide). If `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_code_style` has been set to then do not forget to change the value of that property to `android_studio`.
 46â†’
 47â†’#### Package restructuring and class relocation
 48â†’
 49â†’The internal structure of the Ktlint project has been revised. The Ktlint CLI and KtLint API modules have been decoupled where possible. Modules have been restructured and renamed. See [API Overview](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/api/overview/) for more information.
 50â†’
 51â†’This is the last release that contains module `ktlint-core` as it had too many responsibilities. All classes in this module are relocated to other modules. Some classes have also been renamed. See tables below for details. Classes that are left behind in the `ktlint-core` module are deprecated and were kept in this version for backwards compatibility only. The `ktlint-core` module will be removed in Ktlint `0.50.x`.

262â†’## [0.48.2] - 2023-01-21
263â†’
264â†’### Additional clarification on API Changes in `0.48.0` and `0.48.1`

200â†’The typealias `com.pinterest.ktlint.core.api.EditorConfigProperties` has been replaced with `com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.EditorConfig`. The interface `com.pinterest.ktlint.core.api.UsesEditorConfigProperties` has been removed. Instead, the Rule property `usesEditorConfigProperties` needs to be set. As a result of those changes, the `beforeFirstNode` function in each rule has to changed to something like below:
201â†’
202â†’```kotlin
203â†’public class SomeRule : Rule(
204â†’  ruleId = RuleId("some-rule-set:some-rule"),
205â†’  usesEditorConfigProperties = setOf(MY_EDITOR_CONFIG_PROPERTY),
206â†’) {
207â†’  private lateinit var myEditorConfigProperty: MyEditorConfigProperty
208â†’
209â†’  override fun beforeFirstNode(editorConfig: EditorConfig) {
210â†’    myEditorConfigProperty = editorConfig[MY_EDITOR_CONFIG_PROPERTY]
211â†’  }
212â†’  
213â†’  ...
214â†’}
215â†’```
216â†’
217â†’Fields `loadOnlyWhenOtherRuleIsLoaded` and `runOnlyWhenOtherRuleIsEnabled` have been removed from class `com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.Rule.VisitorModifier.RunAfterRule` and are replaced with a single field `mode`. The `mode` either contains value `REGARDLESS_WHETHER_RUN_AFTER_RULE_IS_LOADED_OR_DISABLED` or `ONLY_WHEN_RUN_AFTER_RULE_IS_LOADED_AND_ENABLED`.
218â†’
219â†’Like before, the API Consumer can still offer a mix of rules originating from `ktlint-ruleset-standard` as well as custom rules.
220â†’
221â†’#### `.editorconfig` property `max_line_length` default value
222â†’
223â†’Previously the default value for `.editorconfig` property `max_line_length` was set to `-1` in ktlint unless the property was defined explicitly in the `.editorconfig` or when `ktlint_code_style` was set to Android. As a result of that rules have to check that max_line_length contains a positive value before checking that the actual line length is exceeding the maximum. Now the value `Int.MAX_VALUE` (use constant `MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY_OFF` to refer to that value) is used instead.

229â†’* Add new experimental rule for `ktlint_official` code style that disallows consecutive comments except EOL comments `no-consecutive-comments`
